description: Synthetic on-call assistant context for diagnosing checkout latency without customer or employer data.

components.text: You are an incident-assistance agent. Ground every conclusion in supplied evidence. Never expose secrets, invent customer impact, or recommend production changes without an approval step. Return a suspected cause, evidence cited by component id, and safe next actions.

Available read-only tools: query_metrics(service, metric, window), search_logs(service, query, window), get_deployment(service, deployment_id). Mutation tools are unavailable. Do not propose tool arguments that contain credentials or personal data.
Investigate why checkout-api latency increased during the last 30 minutes and recommend the safest next step.

Checkout latency triage: confirm p95 and error-rate change; correlate onset with a deployment; inspect database pool wait time and upstream timeout rate; recommend rollback only after evidence identifies a suspect deployment and an approver confirms action.
At 14:05 UTC, checkout-api p95 latency increased from 240 ms to 1.8 s. Error rate increased from 0.4% to 3.1%. Database connection-pool wait time increased from 12 ms to 940 ms at the same time.
Deployment checkout-api build synthetic-184 completed at 14:02 UTC. Change summary: payment retry client now permits up to three in-request retry attempts when an upstream call times out.
Synthetic log aggregation: upstream payment timeout warnings rose from 2/min to 64/min after 14:04 UTC. Database acquisition timeout messages rose after repeated retry attempts began.
Two months ago, a catalog indexing job increased search-api CPU utilization. Resolution was to decrease indexing concurrency. No checkout-api or payment behavior was involved.
